definition: A set of rules attached to resources specifying who can access what and what actions are allowed.
A set of protocols allowing different software applications to communicate securely.
A prolonged targeted cyberattack in which attackers remain undetected for a long period.
A protocol used to map IP addresses to physical MAC addresses in a local network.
Software that detects prevents and removes malicious software from systems.
A framework for testing and validating the security of web applications.

expansion: Adversarial Tactics Techniques and Common Knowledge
definition: MITRE framework for understanding attacker behaviors and methods
Ability to keep critical operations running during/after disruptions.
The process of creating systems to recover operations during and after a crisis.
An attack that exploits weaknesses in older TLS/SSL encryption to help recover sensitive data.
A routing protocol used to exchange routing information between autonomous systems on the internet.
A process to predict and evaluate the impact of disruption to business functions.
Firmware that initializes hardware and starts the computer boot process.

expansion: Browser Reconnaissance & Exfiltration via Adaptive Compression of Hypertext
definition: A web attack that uses HTTP compression to extract secret data from encrypted HTTPS responses.
Attacker system used to manage compromised devices.
A trusted organization that issues digital certificates to verify identity and enable secure communications.
A software tool or service that sits between users and cloud services to enforce security policies.
A block-cipher mode where each plaintext block is combined with the previous ciphertext block before encryption.
Distributed system that delivers content quickly and reliably.
A group that responds to cybersecurity incidents and helps mitigate their effects.
Information tied to credit/debit cardholders.
Automating code build, testing, and deployment.
Creates cybersecurity benchmarks and best practices.
Software used to create, manage, and publish website content.

expansion: Control Objectives for Information and Related Technologies
definition: Framework for IT governance and management.
The main processor that executes instructions and performs calculations for the computer.
A set of best-practice guidelines for managing cybersecurity risk (commonly referring to the NIST Cybersecurity Framework).
Handles and responds to security incidents.
A web attack where users are tricked into performing actions they didn't intend while authenticated.
A standardized identifier for known security vulnerabilities.
A numerical scoring system to rate the severity of software vulnerabilities.
Security testing that scans a running application from the outside to find vulnerabilities.
A type of attack that overwhelms a service with traffic from multiple sources causing disruption or shutdown.
Security feature that blocks code from running in memory regions not designated for execution helping prevent exploits
Protocol that automatically assigns IP addresses and network settings to devices on a network
An email authentication method that verifies a sender's domain using a digital signature to prevent spoofing.
Shared library of code used by multiple programs in Windows allowing modular and efficient software functionality
Tools and strategies used to prevent unauthorized access transmission or leakage of sensitive information.

expansion: Domain Message Authentication Reporting and Conformance
definition: An email validation system that uses SPF and DKIM to prevent phishing and provides reporting.
A naming system that translates human-readable domain names into IP addresses computers use to identify each other.
A method of sending DNS queries over HTTPS to improve privacy and reduce tampering.
Attack that aims to shut down a service or system by flooding it with traffic or triggering resource exhaustion
Restoring IT systems and data after disruptions.
A security solution that monitors endpoints for malicious activity and provides real-time threat response.
A marker that indicates no more data can be read from a file or data stream.
A process that monitors and detects changes to files helping identify unauthorized modifications or corruption.
A digital forensics tool used to acquire, analyze, and report on evidence from computers and storage media.
Standard for transferring files between systems.
Tool for analyzing and debugging program behavior.
An encryption/signing tool used to protect data and verify authenticity using public-key cryptography.
A collection of settings in Windows used to control user and computer configurations in an Active Directory environment.
Software that monitors a single system for suspicious activity or policy violations.
Software that detects and actively blocks malicious behavior on an individual host.
Standard language used to create and format content for web pages
A protocol used to request and transfer web pages over the internet typically on port 80.
A web server daemon (service) that handles HTTP requests and serves web pages.
The encrypted version of HTTP using TLS/SSL to secure web communications.
Cloud-delivered virtualized computing resources.
Framework for ensuring the right individuals access the right resources at the right times
A network protocol used for sending error messages and operational information about IP traffic.
Networked systems used to monitor and control industrial processes often targeted in critical infrastructure attacks.

Critical access control vulnerability occurring when an application uses user-supplied input to directly access internal objects (e.g., database keys, files) without proper authorization checks. Attackers exploit this by manipulating IDs in URLs or parameters to access or modify unauthorized data
A system that monitors network or system activity for signs of security breaches or malicious behavior.
Microsoft's web server platform used to host websites and web applications.
Evidence or artifacts that suggest a system has been breached or is under attack.
The primary protocol for sending data across networks assigning addresses and routing packets.
A system that actively monitors and blocks detected threats or unauthorized activity in real- time.
The process of detecting analyzing and responding to security incidents to minimize damage.
An international body that creates standards for technology including information security (e.g. ISO/IEC 27001).
Company that provides access to the internet for individuals and organizations
Use of computers, networks, and systems for data.
Framework for IT service management best practices.
Lightweight data exchange format.
Metric to measure progress toward goals.
Network covering a small, local area (e.g., office).
A protocol used to access and maintain directory information like usernames and credentials.
Vulnerability allowing execution of local files.
Preliminary agreement document.
A Windows process that enforces security policy and handles user authentication and token creation.
A unique hardware identifier assigned to network interfaces for communications on the physical network.
Cryptographic hash function used for checksums now considered insecure due to vulnerability to collisions
A security method that requires two or more types of credentials for user authentication.
A non-binding agreement that outlines terms and expectations for collaboration between entities.
Security tool for penetration testing and exploitation.
A third-party company that remotely manages a customer's IT infrastructure and end-user systems.
Microsoft's implementation of Remote Procedure Call used for inter-process and network-based service requests.
A third-party vendor that provides outsourced monitoring and management of security systems.
Average time to discover a security incident.
A security solution that enforces policies for device compliance before allowing network access.
Contract protecting confidential information.
An advanced firewall with features like deep packet inspection intrusion prevention and application awareness.
A system that monitors network traffic for signs of malicious activity or policy violations.
Security system that inspects network traffic and blocks threats in real time
US agency that develops cybersecurity standards and guidelines including the NIST Cybersecurity Framework
A protocol used to synchronize clocks between systems over a network critical for accurate logging and security.
A centralized team/location that monitors networks and systems to keep services available and stable.
Standard for security testing.
The core software that manages hardware and software resources and provides services for applications.
Publicly available information (like websites or social media) used for investigative or threat intelligence purposes
Standard for security testing.
A database that catalogs publicly disclosed software vulnerabilities (historically community- driven).
Systems used to control industrial equipment like SCADA often found in manufacturing or utilities.
A manufacturer identifier used in the first part of a MAC address to identify the vendor.
Web app security nonprofit and resources.
Tools and strategies used to monitor and control accounts with elevated access rights to reduce risk.

expansion: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
definition: A set of standards ensuring secure handling of credit card information.
Widely used server-side scripting language.
Unique number assigned to a running process.
Any data that can identify a person such as name SSN or email critical for data privacy.
A framework for managing digital certificates and public-key encryption to support secure communications.
Industrial computer for automation.
Demo showing feasibility of an idea or exploit.
Centralized protocol for authentication authorization and accounting in remote access
Vulnerability enabling remote attacker code execution.
A Microsoft protocol that allows users to remotely access and control a Windows computer.
Web service design style using standard HTTP.
Vulnerability letting attackers load remote files.
A protocol that lets a program request a service or execute a function on another system remotely.
Maximum acceptable amount of data loss measured in time used in disaster recovery planning
Widely used asymmetric encryption algorithm known for secure data transmission.
Target time to restore systems and operations, including data, after a disruption or disaster.
Injection attack bouncing malicious code to a user.
A cloud computing model where applications are delivered over the internet and maintained by a provider.
An XML-based standard used for exchanging authentication and authorization data between parties.
A maturity model used to assess and improve an organization's software security practices.
A cloud-delivered architecture that combines networking and security into one service.
Security testing that analyzes source code (without running it) to find vulnerabilities.
A control system used for industrial processes like power plants and water treatment.
A structured process for planning developing testing and deploying software systems.
An approach that separates the control plane from the data plane for flexible and programmable network management.
A secure version of FTP that uses SSH to encrypt file transfers and protect data in transit.
A centralized solution for collecting analyzing and correlating security data in real time.
A contract between a service provider and customer that outlines performance availability and responsibilities
Performance or reliability target for a service.
Network protocol for sharing files printers and serial ports between nodes on a network
Text messaging service used in mobile devices sometimes leveraged in security notifications or MFA
Protocol used to send email from clients to servers and between servers
Protocol used for exchanging structured information in web services using XML
Protocol used to monitor and manage devices on a network such as routers and switches
A protocol for exchanging structured messages between systems, often using XML over HTTP.
A platform that integrates security tools to automate and streamline incident response.
A centralized team and facility that monitors and manages an organization's security posture.
An email validation system designed to prevent spoofing by verifying sender IPs.
A standard language used for managing and querying databases.
Type of attack where malicious SQL code is inserted into input fields to access or manipulate a database
Protocol that provides secure remote command-line access and encrypted data communication
The broadcast name that identifies a Wi-Fi network.
An older encryption protocol used to secure web communications now deprecated in favor of TLS.
A user authentication process that allows access to multiple systems with one set of credentials.
Attack tricking a server into making requests.
A standardized format for sharing threat intelligence across organizations.
A security solution that filters web traffic to prevent access to malicious websites and enforce policy.
Communication protocols that governs how data is transmitted across networks.
Simple, lightweight file transfer system.
A cryptographic protocol that ensures privacy and data integrity between applications over a network.
FINRA system for reporting bond trades.
A cybersecurity framework describing how attackers operate and organize their behavior.
Connectionless communication protocol used for fast data transmission but with no error correction
Security using behavior patterns to detect threats.
A general identifier used to locate or identify resources on the internet including URLs and URNs.
A specific type of URI that provides the address to access resources on the internet (e.g. https://example.com).
A common interface used to connect peripherals to computers; can be a vector for malware if unmanaged.
The primary global time standard used for consistent timekeeping and synchronization.
A logical grouping of devices on a network segmented by function or department improving security.
A software-emulated computer system that runs an OS and apps independently of the physical host system.
A secure connection over the internet that encrypts data between a user and a private network.
A security filter that monitors and blocks malicious traffic targeting web applications.
Network spanning a large geographic area.
A cybersecurity approach that integrates threat data across endpoints networks and servers for holistic response.
A flexible format for storing and transporting structured data often used in web and security applications
A web vulnerability where attackers inject malicious scripts into websites viewed by other users.

Security approach assumes no implicit trust and requires verification for every access request.
